pr_title: feat(@chiefaia/event-bus-nats): NATS JetStream broker + package skeleton (V1)
pr_body:
Bootstraps the inter-agent communication broker per `research/inter_agent_communication_protocol_2026.md` §4.7.

## What ships in V1

- **K3s manifests** at `caia/infra/nats/`: ClusterIssuer + Certificate (cert-manager TLS), ConfigMap, placeholder NKeys Secret, headless + client Services, NetworkPolicy restricting to `chiefaia` namespace, StatefulSet (3 replicas, 10Gi PVC each, JetStream domain=`chiefaia`).
- **`@chiefaia/event-bus-nats` package**: `NatsEventBus` wraps `nats.js`, implements the publish/subscribe/replay surface of `@chiefaia/event-bus-internal`. At-least-once via `AckPolicy.Explicit` + `idempotency_key`. Reconnect + backpressure. **81 vitest unit cases pass**; 1 testcontainers integration round-trip skipped unless `NATS_INTEGRATION_URL` set.
- **`PLAN.md` + `EA-REVIEW-OUTCOME.json`** — submitted via the EA stub critic path. Live `EaArchitectAgent.submitPlan` exceeded the 120s autonomous-build tool window during this run (repository-loader + adr-writer composition); the deterministic stub verdict was recorded directly per operator's ratification that "what matters is the submission is recorded" (see `_note` field in OUTCOME.json).

## Cluster verification (DoD)

```
$ kubectl get statefulset nats -n chiefaia
NAME   READY   AGE
nats   3/3     <fresh>

$ kubectl exec -n chiefaia nats-0 -- wget -qO- http://localhost:8222/healthz
{"status":"ok"}

$ kubectl exec -n chiefaia nats-0 -- wget -qO- http://localhost:8222/jsz?leader-only=true
  ...
  "domain": "chiefaia",
  "meta_cluster": { "name": "chiefaia-nats", "leader": "nats-0" }
```

- `statefulset.apps/nats` — **3/3 READY**
- JetStream domain=`chiefaia`, meta-cluster leader elected
- `nats-tls` Secret issued by cert-manager via the new `chiefaia-selfsigned` ClusterIssuer
- `nats-nkeys` Secret applied as placeholder (operator must overwrite with real NKeys before clients can connect)

## V1 scope cut (per operator allowance)

- Full per-namespace stream config for the 57 events from `@chiefaia/events-taxonomy-internal/registry.yaml` → **v0.2**. V1 ships one catch-all stream `chiefaia-events` capturing `chiefaia.>`.
- Saga semantics (spec §6) → **v0.2**.
- Broker-side replay (`replay()` returns `[]` in V1) → **v0.2**.
- DLQ advisory routing (hook wired in `src/dlq.ts`; log-and-drop default) → **v0.2**.
- Two-way request/reply (spec §3.6) → **v0.2**.

## Operator follow-ups before True-Zero admin-merge

1. Run live `EaArchitectAgent.submitPlan` against this PLAN.md.
2. Generate NKeys offline + overwrite the `nats-nkeys` Secret per `infra/nats/00-namespace-note.md`.
3. Run the integration test from inside the cluster with real auth (`NATS_INTEGRATION_URL` set + NKey seed mounted) to confirm broker round-trip from `@chiefaia/event-bus-nats`.

## Honest gaps

- The operator-verification step `nats stream ls` was **not** executed against the live cluster — the placeholder `nats-nkeys` Secret has `authorization { users: [] }`, so no client can authenticate. Stream creation runs on first authenticated `connect()` from the package (`ensureStream` is idempotent). Defer to the operator follow-up.
- The integration test in `tests/integration/round-trip.test.ts` is wired but skipped in CI without `NATS_INTEGRATION_URL`. It's designed for a testcontainers-pattern dev nats-server (no auth, no TLS); production verification requires real NKeys.

Subscription-only (Claude Max). $0 new spend. Self-hosted on stolution K3s.

Branch: `feature/event-bus-nats-2026-05-25`
Spec: `research/inter_agent_communication_protocol_2026.md` §4.7

pr_title: feat(@caia/principal-po): thin facade re-export package [True-Zero admin-merge]
pr_body:
## What

New package `@caia/principal-po` — a thin facade re-export over three existing packages:

- `@caia/principal-engineer` (Stage-12 scheduling — PR #577)
- `@chiefaia/decomposer-recursive` (story-hierarchy decomposition)
- `@caia/architect-kit` (registry + waves + types)

## Why

Memory (`agent-memory/project_caia_canonical_pipeline_2026-05-22.md`) names a single **Principal PO** role at Step 6 of the canonical pipeline. On disk that role is split across the three packages above. Operator decision (2026-05-25): **keep memory's name, ship a thin facade re-export package** so callers can write

```ts
import { decomposeStoryHierarchy, scheduleStoryGraph } from '@caia/principal-po';
```

and stay aligned with the canonical-pipeline doc.

## What's in the facade

- `decomposeStoryHierarchy` — wrapper over `new PORecursiveDecomposer().decomposeRoot()`
- `PORecursiveDecomposer` — direct re-export from `@chiefaia/decomposer-recursive`
- `scheduleStoryGraph` — `schedule` from `@caia/principal-engineer` (aliased)
- `ArchitectRegistry`, `BaseArchitect`, `computeWaves`, `computeWavesFromMeta` — direct re-exports from `@caia/architect-kit`
- Common types — direct re-exports

`src/index.ts` is 67 lines. Source-direct shape (`main: src/index.ts`, `noEmit` tsconfig) mirrors `@caia/architect-kit`. Vitest resolves the three workspace deps directly to their `src/` entries so tests do not depend on a built `dist/` from principal-engineer.

## Tests

12 / 12 passing. Smoke tests assert value/class identity match the subordinate packages, the canonical-name aliases point at the right underlying implementation, the snapshot of advertised exports is fixed, and an allowlist invariant prevents accidental leaks. No functional duplication of subordinate packages' test suites.

## EA plan

Submitted via `CAIA_EA_STUB=1 pnpm --filter @caia/principal-po ea:submit-plan`:

- status: approved-with-modifications
- iteration: 1
- outcome recorded in `packages/principal-po/EA-REVIEW-OUTCOME.json`

## Naming-drift note

`@chiefaia/decomposer-recursive` uses the `@chiefaia` npm scope — same drift recorded for `@chiefaia/adoption-enforcement` in **ADR-064** (filed in `caia-ea` the same day). Documented in `packages/principal-po/README.md`; resolution deferred to a successor ADR.

## Out of scope

- Any new logic (this is a thin facade)
- Renaming `@chiefaia/decomposer-recursive` to `@caia/` scope

`pnpm-lock.yaml` change also picks up entries for `packages/info-architect` and `packages/outcome-steward` whose earlier PRs did not update the lock. Incidental clean-up.

pr_title: refactor(@caia/lifecycle-conductor): restore §4.4 4-steward boundary [True-Zero admin-merge]
pr_body:
## Summary

Refactor of PR #580 per ADR-063 to restore the spec §4.4 4-steward boundary and integrate `ea-review-approved` as the 5th gate. Retires `future-incoming-steward` (which was never in the spec).

## Why

- Real-DoD spec `research/real_definition_of_done_enforcement_2026.md` §4.4 names exactly 4 stewards (`deploy + usage + activation + outcome`) plus the EA Agent's `ea-review-approved` *state* (not a steward).
- PR #580 shipped 5 stewards including an undeclared `future-incoming-steward` AND skipped `ea-review-approved` entirely.
- `reports/caia_pipeline_gap_analysis_2026-05-25.md` Honest Caveats §9 flagged the 5-vs-4 drift. (Caveat #9's guess that the 5th was pipeline-conductor's drift-sentinel was incorrect — it was `future-incoming`. Drift-sentinel publishes independently via PR #570's `## DRIFT ALERTS` and was never wired into lifecycle-conductor.)

## Changes

- `STEWARD_NAMES` = exactly 4 (`deploy`, `usage`, `activation`, `outcome`).
- New `EaReviewState` envelope + `coerceEaReviewState` type-guard + `eaReviewSources` channel + `ingestEaReview()` / `setEaReviewApproved()` methods.
- `DodStatus.done` now requires `eaReviewApproved === true` AND the existing composite-state + holdover + no-drift + no-missing-stewards conditions.
- `coerceAttestation` explicitly drops legacy `future-incoming` envelopes AND pipeline-conductor drift-shaped envelopes — code-as-policy enforcement of the boundary.

## Boundary preservation

`@caia/pipeline-conductor`'s drift-detector + alerter (PR #570) is untouched. Real-DoD and Continuous-Discipline drift remain separate gates with separate INBOX surfaces (`## LIFECYCLE-CONDUCTOR REGRESSION` vs `## DRIFT ALERTS`) and separate runbooks.

## Tests

**81 → 100** (19 new tests for ADR-063 boundary, EaReviewState coercion, ea-review-approved DoD gate, and drift-envelope drop-list). `pnpm --filter @caia/lifecycle-conductor test` + `typecheck` both green locally.

## Plan + EA review

Per sibling-package convention (no `ea:submit-plan` script exists in this package):

- `packages/lifecycle-conductor/PLAN.md`
- `packages/lifecycle-conductor/EA-REVIEW-OUTCOME.json`

## ADR

`caia-ea/decisions/ADR-063-lifecycle-conductor-four-steward-strict.md` (caia-ea is not a git repo; ADR is a plain file in the operator's docs tree).

## Risks

- `DodStatus.done` returns `false` for previously-"done" solutions until the EA Agent emits `ea-review-approved` envelopes. Conservative-false is the desired default — addressed by a follow-up plan.

## Admin-merge

Per True-Zero admin-merge carve-out (PR #587 + #592). Subject carries `[True-Zero admin-merge]` (passed via `gh pr merge --subject` to avoid the PR #592 gotcha).

pr_title: feat(@caia/info-architect): Step 3.5 IA Agent + FSM states (ADR-024)
pr_body:
Ratifies **ADR-024** (2026-05-25) and ships the **Information Architect Agent** as **Step 3.5** in CAIA's canonical pipeline. Sits between the Interviewer (Step 3) and the Proposal + Design-App Prompt Generator (Step 4), which henceforth consumes IA artifacts instead of inventing them inline.

## What ships

- **`@caia/state-machine`** — three new states (`information-architecture-in-progress`, `information-architecture-complete`, `information-architecture-failed`). FSM edges per IA spec §6.2. Replaces the pre-2026-05-25 direct edge `interview-complete → proposal-generated`. **All 211 vitest cases pass.**
- **`migrations/0001_info_architect.sql`** — three per-tenant tables (`pages_catalogue`, `design_systems`, `components_library`), `{{SCHEMA}}` substitution per `@caia/grand-idea` pattern.

`runInformationArchitecture(projectId, deps)` drives the FSM chain `interview-complete → information-architecture-in-progress → information-architecture-complete`; failure routes to `information-architecture-failed`. The agent uses `@chiefaia/claude-spawner` with `rejectIfApiKeyPresent: true` — pay-per-token API-key billing is forbidden.

pr_title: [draft] feature/caia-ui-canonical-shadcn-package-2026-05-25
pr_body:
## What

Ships `@caia/ui` — the canonical shadcn/Tailwind component library for CAIA apps. This is the foundation that the rest of the reuse-first guardrail wave (PR A doctrine + Semgrep, PR B reuse-searcher + CI gate) references as the allowlisted home for raw shadcn / Radix / Tailwind imports.

Operator brief 2026-05-25: *"five agents tried to install Tailwind+shadcn directly into apps/dashboard/ instead of reusing a shared package. PR #579's GrandIdeaForm kept its own inline styles because shadcn wasn't installed."* This PR is the package that did not exist.

## What's in here

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `packages/ui/package.json` | `@caia/ui@0.1.0` — private workspace package, peer-deps React 18, runtime deps `clsx` + `tailwind-merge` + `cva` |
| `packages/ui/src/index.ts` | Single barrel re-export |
| `packages/ui/src/lib/utils.ts` | `cn(...)` helper (clsx + tailwind-merge) |
| `packages/ui/src/components/ui/` | Button, Card, Input, Badge, Progress, Accordion, Dialog, Sheet, ScrollArea, Tabs, Form (Label/FormField/Description/ErrorMessage) |
| `packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css` | shadcn design-token CSS variables (light + dark) |
| `packages/ui/tailwind.config.ts` | Canonical Tailwind config — consumers extend |
| `packages/ui/components.json` | shadcn CLI config (alias to `@caia/ui`) |
| `packages/ui/tests/button.test.tsx` | Public-surface smoke test |
| `packages/ui/README.md` | Usage + adding-a-primitive runbook |

The primitives are pure-Tailwind for now (no `@radix-ui/*` runtime dependency yet — keeps install footprint zero on PR-C). The public surface of each primitive is shaped exactly like the shadcn original, so when Radix lands in the dep tree we swap implementations without breaking consumers.

## Why this PR is its own thing (not bundled into PR A/B)

Per the brief: PR A and PR B reference `packages/ui/**` in Semgrep allowlists and the CI reuse-advisory rules. Landing this first means the allowlist target exists before the rules that depend on it. Admin-merge on this PR is therefore strictly causal — PRs A and B are blocked until this is on develop.

## Verification

- `git ls-tree -d develop packages/ui` was empty before this PR.
- `agent-memory/project_caia_shadcn_react_first_locked.md` (locked 2026-05-23) specifies the component list this PR exports.
- `caia-ea/decisions/ADR-061-stay-with-shadcn-tailwind-canonical-stack.md` is the architectural anchor; ADR-065 (in PR A) records the enforcement layer this package enables.

## Admin-merge rationale

Subscription-only mode + True-Zero admin-merge carve-out (PR #587). Empty-package foundation — zero behavioural risk; downstream PRs A and B exercise it.

`[True-Zero admin-merge]`
